pdg_node.statement: (graph: Graph, lattice_instance: LatticeInstance, previously_generated_subgraphs: Collection[Subgraph], seed: int|None) -> MappingContext
pdg_node.informal_desc: Construct immutable algorithm context from constructor inputs so all later stages consume explicit state instead of hidden class fields.

declaration.name: assemblestaticmappingcontext
declaration.type_signature: (graph: Graph, lattice_instance: LatticeInstance, previously_generated_subgraphs: Collection[Subgraph], seed: int|None) -> MappingContext
declaration.docstring: Construct immutable algorithm context from constructor inputs so all later stages consume explicit state instead of hidden class fields.
declaration.conceptual_summary: ImmutableCompositeStateAssembler. Aggregates multiple externally supplied data structures and an optional deterministic control scalar into a single explicit state record. The transform is structural: it preserves each input by reference/value while reifying them as immutable fields, eliminating implicit ambient state. The result is a canonical context object that downstream procedures can consume with fixed, reproducible access semantics.. Applications: Computational biology: package interaction network, folding-topology constraints, prior motif candidates, and optional randomization seed into a reproducible experiment context, Robotics planning: package environment connectivity map, kinematic constraint manifold, prior trajectory fragments, and tie-break seed into a fixed planning context, Distributed systems optimization: package service dependency graph, resource-topology model, prior allocation subplans, and deterministic seed into an immutable scheduling context, Materials simulation: package atomic connectivity structure, lattice/mesh topology instance, previously sampled local configurations, and seed into a reproducible simulation context

pdg_node.statement: (mapping_context: MappingContext, starting_node: NodeId, mapping: Map[GraphNode,LatticeNode], unmapping: Map[LatticeNode,GraphNode], unexpanded_nodes: Set[GraphNode]) -> MappingState
pdg_node.informal_desc: Create initial mapping/unmapping frontier state from a selected starting node.

declaration.name: initializefrontierfromstartnode
declaration.type_signature: (mapping_context: MappingContext, starting_node: NodeId, mapping: Map[GraphNode,LatticeNode], unmapping: Map[LatticeNode,GraphNode], unexpanded_nodes: Set[GraphNode]) -> MappingState
declaration.docstring: Create initial mapping/unmapping frontier state from a selected starting node.
declaration.conceptual_summary: SeedBidirectionalCorrespondenceFrontier. Initializes a search state by anchoring a partial correspondence between two relational structures at a selected source element. It takes an existing forward map, its inverse map, and a set of unexpanded source elements, then returns a new immutable state where correspondence consistency constraints are preserved. The result is a frontier-ready state for subsequent expansion steps in a correspondence-construction process.. Applications: Bootstrapping residue-contact-graph to template-topology alignment in computational biology, Seeding component-netlist to physical-layout correspondence in electronic design automation, Initializing landmark-topology matching between sensed environment graphs and prior maps in robotics, Starting entity-alignment search between heterogeneous knowledge graphs in information systems

pdg_node.statement: (mapping_context: MappingContext, mapping: Map[GraphNode,LatticeNode], unmapping: Map[LatticeNode,GraphNode]) -> bool
pdg_node.informal_desc: Evaluate whether the current mapping/unmapping pair satisfies graph-lattice consistency constraints.

declaration.name: validatecurrentmapping
declaration.type_signature: (mapping_context: MappingContext, mapping: Map[GraphNode,LatticeNode], unmapping: Map[LatticeNode,GraphNode]) -> bool
declaration.docstring: Evaluate whether the current mapping/unmapping pair satisfies graph-lattice consistency constraints.
declaration.conceptual_summary: Bidirectional Relation Consistency Validator. Consumes a candidate pair of forward and reverse mappings between two discrete relational structures and tests whether they satisfy a shared constraint system. It verifies inverse coherence (round-trip consistency) and structural admissibility against topology/order constraints encoded in the context object. The result is a single feasibility predicate indicating whether the mapping pair is a valid structure-preserving correspondence.. Applications: Verifying correspondence between segmented regions in multimodal medical images under anatomical adjacency constraints, Checking consistency of symbol-to-state alignments in compiler intermediate-representation lowering with reverse traceability guarantees, Validating entity alignment between heterogeneous knowledge graphs while preserving ontology hierarchy relations, Confirming mesh-to-skeleton attachment maps in computational biomechanics with inverse lookup and topology constraints
